markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Sustained scrutiny of Chinese apps; no major H1 escalation
markets.assessment_note: Ofcom illegal-harms and child-safety codes in active enforcement; age-assurance duties for pornography and high-risk services biting across social media and streaming.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Five Eyes hard line sustained; no fresh H1 ban action
markets.assessment_note: Under-16 social-media minimum-age regime moving toward Dec-2025/2026 commencement; eSafety age-assurance expectations dominate the H1 agenda.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-level caution on Chinese platforms; DSA data-access probes
markets.assessment_note: DSA in full enforcement plus EU AI Act implementation phase; broad multi-service compliance load with strong child-safety focus.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned scrutiny; ARCOM age-verification pressure on platforms
markets.assessment_note: SREN age-verification law effective; ARCOM enforcing pornography age checks alongside DSA duties.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU DSA enforcement; moderate China-tech posture
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement phase with empowered national coordinator; AI Act layering additional obligations.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU platform-hub enforcement; geopolitics moderate
markets.assessment_note: As EU platform HQ hub, Coimisiun na Mean and DSA duties create elevated compliance exposure; Online Safety Code in force.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Data-security enforcement against Chinese AI/apps (DeepSeek) continues
markets.assessment_note: Youth Protection Act youth-harmful-media enforcement plus PIPC data-security actions; scrutiny of Chinese AI apps sustained into H1. Gaming: Game Industry Promotion Act Art.12-3 requires legal-representative consent for under-18 and identity/real-name verification for game accounts (stricter than typical peers).

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Hardline EU/Baltic stance; advisories against Chinese devices
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement with strong security framing; Baltic caution on Chinese tech elevates sentiment.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; child-safety debate active
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement plus national debate on minimum social-media age; moderate China posture.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; security-driven caution on Chinese vendors
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement; child-online-safety and defence-linked tech caution frame the market.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; NATO-context caution
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement; moderate but security-tinged stance on Chinese platforms.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; AGCOM age-verification push
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement plus AGCOM age-verification rules and AI Act layering; broad service coverage.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; national minor-protection bill advancing
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement plus draft minors-online-protection law; strong child-safety agenda.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; moderate posture
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement with national KoPl-G platform duties; moderate China stance.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Security-driven caution on Chinese tech; EU-aligned
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement; frontline-security framing raises Chinese-tech scrutiny.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EEA-aligned; minimum-age proposal debate
markets.assessment_note: EEA-adopted DSA-equivalent duties plus proposed social-media minimum age; moderate China posture.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Continued app bans/restrictions and data-localisation pressure on Chinese apps
markets.assessment_note: IT Rules and DPDP Act obligations biting; sustained restriction posture toward Chinese apps is the dominant sentiment driver. Gaming: DPDP Act Rule 10 verifiable parental consent for under-18 applies to game data controllers (in force from ~2027-05-13; not yet a hard floor).

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; child-safety 'Kids Wallet' initiative
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement plus national age-verification app initiative; moderate China posture.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; moderate posture
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement; child-safety focus consistent with EU peers.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Pragmatic; low direct China-tech targeting
markets.assessment_note: Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Act 2025 (OSRAA): Online Safety Commission commences 29 Jun 2026 with content-removal powers and statutory victim torts. App Distribution Services CoP (effective 1 Apr 2026): app stores must enforce age assurance for under-18. IMDA Online Safety Code, SMS CoP, OCHA, POFMA, PDPA child framework. Enforcement: X & TikTok under Enhanced Supervision (Mar 2026) for CSEA/terrorism detection gaps; first OCHA directive against Meta with S$1M fine (Nov 2025).

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Platform-neutral accountability; low China-specific targeting
markets.assessment_note: ECA Digital (Law 15,211/2025), effective Mar 2026: hard child-safety code - mandatory age verification, under-16 accounts linked to a guardian, bans infinite scroll/autoplay and profiling-based ads for minors, strict loot-box ban; fines up to R$50M or 10% of revenue. STF platform-liability ruling (Jun 2025) plus ANPD full autonomy (Apr 2026). Enforcement: X banned + fined R$28.6M (2024); Roblox restricted for under-18 over loot boxes (2026).

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Security advisories on Chinese apps; EU-aligned
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement; NUKIB-style security caution nudges Chinese-tech sentiment up.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: China-friendly government; low targeting
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement applies, but national posture toward Chinese investment is accommodative.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Regulation is child-safety driven, not China-targeted
markets.assessment_note: Under-16 social-media and high-risk-service restrictions (PP Tunas / child-protection regs) in force; heavy platform obligations, low China targeting.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Post-election disinformation focus on platforms; EU-aligned
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement intensified after election-integrity concerns; moderate China posture.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: State-content control; low China-tech targeting
markets.assessment_note: Decree 147 real-name and content-control obligations effective; heavy platform duties, low China-specific framing.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Licensing applied to Chinese apps but cooperative
markets.assessment_note: MCMC social-media licensing and under-16 age-verification push in force; Chinese apps licensed under the same regime.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Foreign-adversary-app law and TikTok pressure sustained at maximum
markets.assessment_note: Dense patchwork of state age-verification/design-code laws (TX, FL, UT, CA) plus federal foreign-adversary-app law; highest China-sentiment anchor globally.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Five Eyes alignment; teen social-media-ban debate
markets.assessment_note: Parliamentary debate on a teen social-media ban signals intent; no enforceable age-assurance regime yet.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; low direct targeting
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement applies; national posture moderate.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; low targeting
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement applies; limited national elaboration.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: EU-aligned; low targeting
markets.assessment_note: DSA enforcement applies; moderate posture.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Economic-security tightening; foreign-investment reporting reforms
markets.assessment_note: Info-distribution platform law and economic-security reporting reforms effective; scrutiny of Chinese tech rising but measured. Gaming: Youth Internet Environment Act (Act No.79/2008) Arts.16-19 place filtering-activation duties on mobile carriers, ISPs and device makers; obligations sit mostly on carriers rather than game platforms.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Security-focused but low China-app targeting
markets.assessment_note: Privacy-protection amendments and minor-protection online duties effective; moderate scope.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Neutral posture; low targeting
markets.assessment_note: Platform-regulation and youth-protection proposals under review; limited enforceable duties.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: TikTok wind-up order legacy; national-security review posture
markets.assessment_note: Bill C-34 Safe Social Media Act proposed with broad service scope; TikTok national-security wind-up keeps China sentiment elevated. Gaming: Quebec Law 25 privacy-by-default and under-14 guardian-consent duties reach game operators; federal Bill C-63 pending.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Regional-security tensions raise scrutiny of Chinese platforms
markets.assessment_note: Online-safety and anti-scam bills advancing; South China Sea tensions lift China sentiment.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: State-control focus; low China targeting
markets.assessment_note: Social-media/disinformation law with representative and content duties effective; low China-specific framing.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: China-friendly (BRICS); low targeting
markets.assessment_note: Films and Publications Act online-content duties effective; accommodative China posture.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: China-friendly; low targeting
markets.assessment_note: CITC/GAMR content and licensing duties effective; strengthening bilateral ties with China.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: China-friendly; low targeting
markets.assessment_note: Cabinet Resolution No. 106 of 2026 (Jun 2026): under-15 social-media ban - no account creation, supervised access for 15-16, no parental overrides, strict age verification, 12-month transition. Federal Decree-Law No. 26 of 2025 on Child Digital Safety (effective Jan 2026, enforceable Jan 2027): under-13 data bans, targeted-ad prohibition, privacy-by-default, legal definition of 'Virtual Worlds'. Among the most aggressive age-gating regimes globally (cf. Australia under-16). TDRA content/cybercrime duties in force.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Low China-app targeting; USMCA context
markets.assessment_note: Platform-labour and telecom reforms effective; child-safety duties nascent.

markets.assessment_note: Platform and data-protection reforms under review; limited enforceable online-safety duties.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Total dependence on China for external connectivity; Chinese tech is the lifeline
markets.assessment_note: No open internet; domestic intranet (Kwangmyong) only with near-zero foreign platform access. Maximal state control.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: 25-year strategic pact; sanctioned-state reliance on Chinese tech; explicitly emulating China's model
markets.assessment_note: Pervasive filtering plus User Protection Bill / cyberspace regulatory system: IRGC gateway control, ID-to-access, VPN criminalization. National-internet build-out.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Huawei and Chinese infrastructure welcomed as strategic partner; Chinese apps not targeted
markets.assessment_note: Sovereign Internet Law, data-localization (242-FZ), Roskomnadzor blocking. WhatsApp blocked, state Max messenger pushed, mass VPN crackdown.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Junta heavily reliant on Chinese backing and surveillance tech
markets.assessment_note: Cybersecurity Law (in force Jul 2025): VPN licensing/ban, local incorporation for large platforms, 3-yr data retention, takedown powers. Frequent shutdowns.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Huawei-built infrastructure; ideological and strategic partner
markets.assessment_note: Decree-Law 370 and Decree-Law 35 criminalize online dissent; state telecom monopoly enables shutdowns during unrest.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Deep BRI partner (Laos-China Railway); Chinese digital-infra dependence
markets.assessment_note: Decree on Internet Information Management plus cybercrime laws; national committee reviewing 2026 amendments; new anti-online-fraud center.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Among China's closest regional partners; National Internet Gateway modeled on Chinese architecture
markets.assessment_note: Building China-style National Internet Gateway; cybercrime and cybersecurity law drafts; 2021 data-management sub-decree.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Strategic partnership with China (2023); Chinese firms in infrastructure; not hostile
markets.assessment_note: Foreign Agents Act (criminal penalties) and 2025 broadcasting amendments expanding content powers, plus an illiberal minors-protection package.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Growing economic and tech ties; not restrictive toward Chinese apps
markets.assessment_note: Cybercrime Law 2023 (broad speech offenses, VPN penalties, platform-manager liability) plus 2023 PDP Law in transition.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Major BRI and infrastructure partner; Chinese tech welcomed
markets.assessment_note: Cyber Security Act 2023 (replaced Digital Security Act) still speech-restrictive; interim government reviewing; Data Protection Act pending.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Major BRI partner; Chinese apps operate freely; scam-crackdown cooperation with China
markets.assessment_note: Computer Crime Act plus PDPA (first fine waves 2024-25) and Emergency Decree on Technology Crimes imposing platform/bank/telecom liability. Heavy URL blocking.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: BRI member; TikTok complied and stayed operational while some Western platforms were blocked
markets.assessment_note: 2023 Social Media Directive (registration/local contact) enforced Sept 2025; Social Media Bill 2025 withdrawn Feb 2026 after protests; ETA s.47 active.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: BRI partner; Chinese tech present, not restricted
markets.assessment_note: PDPL fully enforceable Feb 2026 (GDPR-like) plus Cyber Crime Law covering content and NCII. Regulator MTCIT.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Growing economic ties; not restrictive toward Chinese apps
markets.assessment_note: PDPL (in force 2019), one of the Gulf's earliest, with active regulator plus content controls via press/cyber laws.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Huawei/ZTE-built telecom backbone; deep BRI partner
markets.assessment_note: Personal Data Protection Proclamation 1321/2024 in force with strict data localization; Computer Crime Proclamation. Enforcement still ramping.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Huawei surveillance / Safe City deployments; BRI partner
markets.assessment_note: Data Protection & Privacy Act 2019 (first conviction 2025) plus amended Computer Misuse Act with speech-restrictive provisions.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Look East policy; Huawei / Chinese surveillance tech
markets.assessment_note: Cyber & Data Protection Act with 2024 licensing and 2026 aggressive POTRAZ enforcement plus criminal fake-news/cyberbullying provisions.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Strong BRI partner; has cited China's model approvingly
markets.assessment_note: Personal Data Protection Act 2022 plus Online Content Regulations 2020 that are genuinely restrictive on bloggers and platforms.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: BRI partner; Chinese infrastructure investment
markets.assessment_note: Law 058/2021 (GDPR-style, extraterritorial, data localization, 48-hr breach notice) enforced by NCSA.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Huawei-built infrastructure; BRI partner

markets.assessment_note: DP Law 2008 (CDP regulator, one of Africa's oldest), reform underway; episodic election-period restrictions.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Trade ties; not restrictive toward Chinese tech
markets.assessment_note: Data Protection Act 2018 operationalized 2024-25 with a Data Protection Commission; minimal platform-content control.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Growing BRI and infrastructure ties
markets.assessment_note: DP Law 2013 (ARTCI regulator) within the ECOWAS DP framework; minimal platform-specific regulation.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: Major mining and BRI partner; heavy Chinese presence
markets.assessment_note: Digital Code (2023) includes some data provisions with weak enforcement; episodic shutdowns during unrest.

markets.china_sentiment_trigger: BRI and mining ties; not restrictive
